column1: # Insulin (preproinsulin) across vertebrates — conservation tracks protein
# Preproinsulin is signal peptide - B chain - C-peptide - A chain. The B and A
# chains fold into mature insulin and are tightly conserved; the C-peptide,
# which is cleaved out and discarded, drifts freely. With relativeTo=Human the
# B and A regions render mostly as dots while the C-peptide region fills with
# letters — conservation mapping onto which parts of the precursor survive into
# the functional hormone. Guinea-pig insulin is famously divergent throughout.
# Columns: UniProt accession <TAB> row label. Human first (reference).
